circular.title: Master Circular - Prudential Norms on Capital Adequacy - Primary (Urban) Co-operative Banks (UCBs)
circular.summary: This Master Circular consolidates the prudential norms regarding capital adequacy for Urban Co-operative Banks (UCBs). It establishes a tiered regulatory framework based on deposit size and geographical reach. UCBs are mandated to maintain minimum Capital to Risk-Weighted Assets Ratios (CRAR) of 9% or 12% depending on their tier, alongside specific minimum net worth requirements. The circular details the components of Tier I and Tier II capital, provides a comprehensive list of risk weights for various asset classes, and sets guidelines for the issuance of regulatory capital instruments.

clauses.text: No co-operative bank shall carry on business unless the aggregate value of paid-up capital and reserves is at least one lakh rupees.
clauses.summary: Minimum capital requirement for co-operative banks

clauses.text: UCBs shall have minimum net worth as under: * Tier 1 UCBs operating in a single district: ₹2 crore. * All other UCBs (all tiers): ₹5 crore.
clauses.summary: Minimum net worth requirements for UCBs

clauses.text: Tier 1 UCBs must maintain a minimum CRAR of 9% of Risk Weighted Assets (RWAs). Tier 2 to 4 UCBs must maintain a minimum CRAR of 12% of RWAs.

obligations.text: Maintain minimum CRAR of 9% (Tier 1) or 12% (Tier 2 to 4) on an ongoing basis.
obligations.plain_english: UCBs must maintain a minimum capital to risk-weighted assets ratio

obligations.text: Achieve phased CRAR of 10% by March 2024, 11% by March 2025, and 12% by March 2026.
obligations.plain_english: Tier 2 to 4 UCBs must meet phased CRAR targets

obligations.text: Maintain minimum net worth of ₹2 crore (single-district Tier 1) or ₹5 crore (all others).
obligations.plain_english: UCBs must maintain minimum net worth

prohibitions.text: Borrowings (except against FDs) must be linked to shareholdings.
prohibitions.plain_english: UCBs must link borrowings to shareholdings

definitions.definition: All unit UCBs and salary earners’ UCBs (irrespective of size), and other UCBs with deposits up to ₹100 crore.
Paid-up capital plus free reserves and other eligible items minus intangible assets and losses.
Capital to Risk-Weighted Assets Ratio, computed as Eligible Total Capital divided by Total Risk-Weighted Assets.
